blocks.en: Radio is a broadcast medium: when one device transmits, everyone in range hears it. If two transmit at once on the same channel, the signals overlap and both are usually lost — a collision.
blocks.zh: 无线电是一种广播介质：一台设备发送时，范围内所有设备都能听到。若两台设备在同一信道上同时发送，信号就会重叠，通常两者都丢失——这就是冲突。

blocks.en: There are two big ideas for sharing: take turns in time, or split the channel into pieces so several devices fit at once. Modern Wi-Fi does both.
blocks.zh: 共享有两个核心思想：在时间上轮流，或把信道切成小块让多台设备同时容纳。现代 Wi-Fi 两者并用。

blocks.en: Taking turns is the older, simpler idea: before talking, a device listens; if the air is busy, it waits its turn. That is the heart of the 802.11 Core track and the next big lesson, CSMA/CA.
blocks.zh: 轮流是更早、更朴素的思想：发送前先聆听；若信道忙，就等到轮到自己。这正是“802.11 核心”轨道的精髓，也是下一节重点 CSMA/CA。

blocks.en: Splitting the channel is called OFDMA. The channel's subcarriers are grouped into Resource Units (RUs), and different users are assigned different RUs — so they transmit at the same time without colliding.
blocks.zh: 把信道切块称为 OFDMA。信道的子载波被分组成资源单元（RU），不同用户被分配不同的 RU——于是他们同时发送而不碰撞。

blocks.en: Why bother splitting at all? Small, frequent messages — a smart plug's status, a game's controller updates — waste a whole channel if each takes a full turn. Giving each a slice lets many finish together, which is far more efficient when a network is busy with many devices.
blocks.zh: 为什么要切分？智能插座的状态、游戏手柄的更新这类小而频繁的消息，如果各占一整次发送机会就太浪费了。给每个分一小片，就能让许多消息一起完成——当网络上设备众多、十分繁忙时，这要高效得多。

blocks.en: Taking turns and splitting the channel are complementary: turns decide who goes when; OFDMA lets several go together within one turn. Later tracks build on both.
blocks.zh: 轮流与切分信道是互补的：轮流决定谁在何时发送；OFDMA 则让若干设备在一次发送机会内同时进行。后续轨道在两者之上展开。
